channel.title: Comments for page "In the Trenches with the Dead"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "In the Trenches with the Dead"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was very lukewarm about MTF omega-zero in its first installment. It felt rather cheap and ad-hoc.

I am now absolutely convinced, it's awesome and a great part of the antimemetics hub. Great, great job. It deals with the "explaining" part of 3125, and fits in extremely well with the overall foundation tone.

I thought 2211 was derivative, but this ? This is a masterpiece and a great addition. You absolutely nailed it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I had the same question.

item.title: Grammar?
item.content:encoded:
[Dying from mnestic overdose, two hundred metres underground, cared for by no one known to exist to no one. No one except a handful of meaningless leftovers soon to be part of an immortal, unkillable idea.]

Shouldn't a comma go between 'cared for by no one' and 'known to exist to no one'

item.title: Doing the maths
item.content:encoded:
27% of 3500 is 945. As around 30 personnel have been lost, this leaves 915.
According to Wikipedia, a battalion is around 700-800. We have a large battalion strength force holding the line.
Does that mean that this is the Eternity Battalion or some shit? Idk, I want a cool name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some combination of the following:

  • Memory is complex and does not necessarily need to be conscious.
  • Marion has extensive training in perfect recall and cognitive compartmentalization.
  • She remembered him when he died, even if she didn't a few minutes later.
  • For all their attempts to systemicize it, there's a lot about ghosts that Omega-Zero doesn't always understand

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How can Marion be Lyn's anchor if

It's weeks later still that Wheeler discovers the largest new hole in her memory:
Who founded the division? When?

item.title: Re: Audio player
item.content:encoded: Weird.

item.title: Re: Audio player
item.content:encoded: I can see the audio player just fine.

item.title: Audio player
item.content:encoded: I guess the audio player is only visible to me? Can anybody confirm this? Does anybody know how to fix it?

item.title: Audio tag
item.content:encoded: Since the tag team hasn't gotten to it, and it's been a bit, I added the audio tag. Hopefully that's right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn. Poor Smith. We hardly knew ye, and then we hardly knew ye again.

Using previously established agents is a brilliant move. For me, certainly, it immediately forms attachment to them. Such history, proven skills, and stories. It makes it more tragic when they must go away, like Smith.

Please keep it up! We'll be looking forward to the next one, eagerly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay, more stuff! Thank you for this, Pudding!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even death could not stop them.

Saints Cooper and Desai will return in "A Thin Dangerous Line".

Coming April 2nd to an internet capable device near you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dear god please finish this series I can't handle the lack of closure!

Amazingly written though. The music, and the forethought, pull you in.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: gib moar

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As the guy whose job it is to curate the MTF list, I can say for certain that Omega-0 is among the most unique and interesting MTF concepts ever created. I'm thrilled to see more of them, especially in this scenario where they're most suited to intervention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like, the ending, the 3125 just sort of pops up.

It injects into Smith and then she tries to dethread Desai, but he eventually rallies. Then it dethreads her completely and takes her place in order to taunt Marness (at the same time that it is taunting Wheeler in Quick-Space).

And it all seems like a big, garbled mess of just talking.

After Cooper and Desai demanifest, it is just talking.

Instead of usuing actual actions

Well none of the "action" is physical.

like the common italicized text between dialogue to display what's happening,

The italicized text is what is visible on the camera feeds, not what is happening in the noosphere.

it seems like your trying to convey what's going on purely through dialogue and it seems odd. Does that make sense?

Well, yes. That's what I said upthread.

The problem is that a bunch of goofy technobabble descriptions of purely conceptual events wouldn't have made it any clearer, and don't make sense in this format. Just like a tactical shooter doesn't say, "I am aiming, I am squeezing the trigger, I am double-tapping, and I am shifting my aim to the head and firing again" they just do it, an identity warrior doesn't say "I am entangling my threads with theirs, based first on common elements, and now I am injecting the package into their narrative, now I am ending entanglement" they just do it. Besides the less of that kind of mumbo-jumbo the better, since it is sure to be total nonsense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like, the ending, the 3125 just sort of pops up. And it all seems like a big, garbled mess of just talking. You can't really tell whats going on and what they're doing, and it feels awkward that way. Instead of usuing actual actions, like the common italicized text between dialogue to display what's happening, it seems like your trying to convey what's going on purely through dialogue and it seems odd. Does that make sense?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While Dr. Mo O'Brien is a certified combat epistemologist, Omega-Zero is composed of combat teleologists; informational constructs who use their purpose and identity as a weapon against other informational constructs.

More to the point, this is really good—a good example of narrative dexterity being used to make complex concepts accessible and just plain fun to read about. Neat touches of capsule characterisation also give this a lot of nice richness that balances the more technobabble-laden sections out.

There's still plenty of untread ground in the field of antimemetics, and this is inspiring me to think about what's going on with the other division that could fight this threat. Gladly upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am really glad that you, ARD and qntm all enjoyed this, it is always a risk when you use someone else's characters that you will get them wrong.

Speaking of which I need to ask you a question about the current state of the Sagrada Familia (which will make an appearance in part IV). Expect a PM soon.

New headcanon: SCP-2521 is simply another facet/incarnation of 3125. After all, 2+5+2+1 = 5+5, or in reverse it’s 1252.

Yeah that's certainly possible, and the fact that it appears to made of dead memes in the form of VHS actually fits into the way that the metaphysics works here. I definitely will think about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really terrific. Sorry I didn’t get to comment on the draft, but I don’t think it needed it. I love the cleverly-constructed technobabble, the braided plot, the brisk but vivid character work, the references - all of it. This is a worthy companion to the Antimemetics series.

This system is able to detect and perceive unsecured info-streams that include information about itself. Whenever (and wherever) this information is detected an incursion occurs

New headcanon: SCP-2521 is simply another facet/incarnation of 3125. After all, 2+5+2+1 = 5+5, or in reverse it’s 1252.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't know I was waiting for this but here I am reading through the whole shebang!

item.title: Now like I wanted it!
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Croquembouche, I fixed the player so that it a) is inside the manifestation log and b) is html5 and will run without flash!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This makes me happy because I'd been wondering for a while how ω-0 was getting involved with the rather important 3125 problem, and the implications towards the end are interesting…

Also I kinda like that 55's dialogue has the same structure in both appearances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Every member of Revenant-3 died in some prior work.

In fact, except for Sanchez my goal is to use only characters who have an established death elsewhere in all MTF ω-0 tales.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Zoe Smith… From On Mount Golgotha?
EDIT: Derp, didn't read the author post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even the ending?

I'm sorry, I don't understand the question. What about the ending?

Because I know what's going on (sort of) and like what happens, but it's the actions that aren't clear and that are difficult to make out.

Well, IWT is not something that makes sense for physical entities at all and since I decided to do this entirely epistlatory, for some reason, there wasn't anyway to do much exposition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even the ending? Because I know what's going on (sort of) and like what happens, but it's the actions that aren't clear and that are difficult to make out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. With qntm taking his time writing Antimemetics Division, it's nice enough someone else has taken interest in writing for it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, when I was crazy enough to try to write an entirely epistlatory action story which was mostly technobabble dialog and the characters are using complex weaponized metaphysics in a way which it is difficult to even comprehend much less describe, I set it up to be a confusing read.

No one is more shocked that this actually still kind of works than I am.

I can tell you that the next installment will have traditional third person omniscient narrative bits, and more exposition, if you can bear with me until then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love takes like these. Absolutely my favorite on the site. Few problems though. Throughout, it can be fairly confusing. It doesn't seem like enough is explained, such as the abilities that they will, or what they're capable of, how they do it, etc. Ending is also sort of all over the place. Sort of difficult to tell what's going on until it gets near the end. +1 though, because I want to see more stories like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
2 of my top 5 fave things in the site together. This hyped the hell out of me as oon as I realized what it was, and did not dsappoint.

Now, we need a 3002-3125 NOW!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More Omega-Zero woot!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
MARLEY was dead: to begin with. … There is no doubt that Marley was dead. This must be distinctly understood, or nothing wonderful can come of the story I am going to relate.
— Charles Dickens, A Christmas Carol

This begins the story of the secret army of the Foundation's restless dead and their desperate and unknown fight to save independent human thought.

If you haven't read SCP-2111, wherein Mobile Task Force Omega-Zero ("Ará Orún") was first introduced, then this may make little sense. Additionally this tale occurs in parallel to CASE COLOURLESS GREEN and Your Last First Day the penultimate and final installments of There is No Antimemetics Division by qntm, which may be found in its entirety at the Antimemetics Division Hub.

+ Credits Where Due
– hide block
  • Lyn Marness and Marion Wheeler were created by qntm and may be found in the Antimemetics Division material linked above.
  • Santosh Desai was created by A Random Day and may be found in the Third Law tale Moonlighting and the Resurrection tale I Thought You Died Alone
  • Zoe Smith was created by Myriad in SCP-1638 and the silence left in her absence is loudly heard in the On Mount Golgotha tales.
  • Riley Cooper was created by psul in SCP-3512 where she earned every ounce of rage.
  • Zoe Smith's music is a selection from Antonín Dvořák's Symphony No. 8 in G major, Op. 88, B. 163, performed by DuPage Symphony Orchestra and conducted by Barbara Schubert and is available under CC from Wikimedia Commons.
+Thanks to
– hide block

qntm for not only letting me play in his sandbox but for generously assisting me in building a haunted wing for his castle.
9Volt for feedback.
Croquembouche for helping me with the coding to get the html5 audio player like I wanted it.

Commentary now on my author page. The IWT Primer in the Foundation Universe section may also be useful.
